name: Housing Opportunities of Northern Delaware, Inc.
description_short: Housing Opportunities of Northern Delaware provides HUD-approved housing counseling, fair housing education, and financial literacy services to help Delaware residents access affordable housing and understand their rights.
description_long:
Housing Opportunities of Northern Delaware, Inc. (HOND) is a non-profit organization dedicated to advancing fair and equal access to housing across Delaware. The organization has been operating long enough to host a 42nd annual event, indicating decades of community presence and commitment to housing advocacy. HOND serves individuals and families facing housing discrimination, financial instability, and barriers to homeownership throughout the state.

The organization offers comprehensive housing counseling services that guide clients through homeownership decisions, financial literacy, credit and budget counseling, default prevention support, and crisis-related housing concerns. They also provide fair housing education through outreach, workshops, forums, and one-to-one guidance to help people understand their housing rights and options. Additionally, HOND supports tenants, landlords, and aspiring homeowners with specialized counseling tailored to different housing stakeholder groups. Their services address discrimination, foreclosure prevention, and pathways to stable, affordable housing.

HOND distinguishes itself through its focus on advocacy and community partnership. The organization actively works with community leaders, partner organizations, and supporters to strengthen neighborhoods and promote housing equity across Delaware. Their annual Fair Housing Observance & Award Luncheon honors leaders advancing fair housing and generates funds to sustain their educational and counseling programs. The organization's long-standing presence and formal recognition structure suggest deep institutional knowledge and established relationships with Delaware housing authorities and community stakeholders.

As a non-profit free-help provider, HOND's primary limitation is that their services are geographically restricted to Delaware residents. While the website confirms they offer HUD-approved housing counseling and fair housing education—both hallmarks of legitimate non-profit housing assistance—specific pricing, eligibility requirements, and detailed service limitations are not outlined in the available content. Prospective clients should contact them directly to confirm service availability and any potential income or residency-based qualifications.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling services providing legitimate, government-endorsed guidance
Comprehensive approach addressing multiple aspects of housing stability including credit, budget, and default prevention
Fair housing advocacy and discrimination support for families facing housing inequity
Established 42-year history demonstrated through long-running annual awards luncheon
One-to-one education available in addition to group workshops and forums
Services for all stakeholder groups including tenants, landlords, and aspiring homeowners
Free-help model as non-profit organization with no for-profit lending or predatory revenue model
cons: Services limited to Delaware residents only, not available nationwide
Website does not specify income eligibility limits, application process, or wait times
Limited detail on counselor credentials, certifications, or staff qualifications beyond HUD-approval
No information about hours of operation, availability of remote counseling, or response times
Fundraising-focused event content takes up significant website real estate rather than service details
best_for: Delaware residents seeking fair housing education and understanding their housing rights
Homebuyers and aspiring homeowners needing counseling on mortgages and financial preparation
Renters and tenants facing discrimination or seeking guidance on tenant rights
Individuals at risk of foreclosure or default needing crisis counseling and prevention support
diagnosis: HOND is best for Delaware residents seeking free, legitimate HUD-approved housing counseling and fair housing education to navigate homeownership, understand tenant rights, or address housing discrimination. The main caveat is that services are restricted to Delaware and specific details about eligibility, response times, and counselor availability require direct contact with the organization.
services: Default prevention support and foreclosure counseling
Community workshops and forums on housing topics
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
